 Brucea javanica, also known as Hamosa bruceae, is a plant species in the family Burseraceae, native to tropical Asia. Brucea javanica has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown it to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Brucea javanica include limonoids, particularly bruceantin and bruceine, which have been found to exhibit anti-tumor and anti-inflammatory activities. Brucea javanica is commonly used in traditional medicine to make various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
